The Municipal Treasurer should deposit intact all collections with the authorized depository bank daily or on the next banking day pursuant to Section 32 of the NGAS Manual Volume I, and Section 134 of GAAM Volume I.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management speed up the implementation of said projects and programs funded under the Bottom-Up Budgeting (BuB) Funds and ensure that funds are optimally utilized with a view of ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the utilization of government resources for the benefit of its constituencies.
observations.managementAction: Substantially implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the general services office conduct a complete
physical inventory count of properties, prepare and submit to the auditor an
updated inventory report on the physical count of the properties not later than
January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124, Volume I of the NGAS
Manual. Continued failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the
reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their
salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission
by virtue of Par. 2, Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the management specifically those in charged with the
local government’s fiscal responsibility conduct a thorough evaluation on the
conditions and constraints of each fund that restricted its timely implementation
and accordingly adopt appropriate remedial measures for the proper and speedy
disposition of said funds in accordance with the respective terms and conditions
of the source agencies.

We recommended that the municipality exert more efforts in the retrieval
of loan documents to establish its validity and propriety and find ways to collect
from the borrowers who have loans outstanding balance as of yearend. We also
recommend that the management make assessment on the collectability of the
loan account and provide for an allowance for impairment losses, if warranted.

We recommended that the management direct the municipal accountant
to deduct the ten percent retention money during progress payments for
infrastructure projects or demand appropriate substitute irrevocable standby
letters of credit, bank guarantees or surety bonds of amounts equivalent to the
retention money in accordance with Annex E of RA 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended that the municipal treasurer prepare and submit report
on receivables to the municipal accountant as basis in the proper recording and
monitoring of the receivables.

We also recommended that the municipal accountant record and reflect the
necessary adjusting journal entry to recognize the amount of accumulated
uncollected fees and to present fairly the financial statements in accordance with the
Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.

The proposed adjusting journal entries to record the recognition of
receivables in the books is shown below:
Dr. Accounts Receivable 2,046,115.98
Cr. Prior Period Adjustment 2,046,115.98

If there is an objective evidence that part of the accounts receivables are
impaired, the entry below is suggested:
Dr. Impairment Loss – Loans and Receivables xxxx
Cr. Allowance for Impairment–Accounts Receivable xxxx

We recommended that the management execute the required MOA
outlining the standards of the project’s implementation and subsequently
monitor compliance thereof in accordance with such standards pursuant to the
rules and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2007-001.

We recommended that the municipal accountant rectify the
aforementioned deficiencies by making the appropriate adjusting journal entries
to reflect the correct balance of each affected accounts and henceforth, adhere to
the requirements of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS)
for accuracy on financial reporting.

We recommended that the municipal accountant prepare the appropriate
adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balance and henceforth, adhere to
the requirements of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS)
for accuracy on financial reporting.

We recommended that the municipal accountant prepare the appropriate
adjusting journal entries and for the concerned officials to conduct periodic
inventory taking and reconciliation of accounting and inventory records for
accuracy on financial reporting.

We recommended that the management retrieve the supporting
documents of the dormant accounts payable and to examine and review such
documents. Further, communicate with the claimants for possible payment if
the transactions are valid and regular. For those with incomplete documents,
require the requesting department/user to submit the same as evidence of the
claim, otherwise the claim will be reverse to its appropriate account.

We recommended that the management submit justification for the
inordinate proceeding, otherwise responsible persons should be held accountable
for violating the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and
Regulations.

Furthermore, we recommended that the management exert due diligence
in the conduct of its procurement proceedings and ensure that procedural rules
are strictly adhered to avoid personal liability.

We recommended that the management submit a justification on the said
observation and to intensify/expedite the hiring or promotion of qualified
personnel to enhance its services and attain full implementation of programs,
plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the
constituents as well as to provide equal opportunities to the deserving
individuals in the community and likewise adhere to the pertinent Civil Service
rules and regulations on the hiring of job order workers.

We recommended that the management submit a justification on the said
observation and to intensify the hiring of qualified personnel in order that the
functions and responsibilities thereof be discharged in accordance with Joint
Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the disbursing officer update the cashbook daily
and accomplished the certification as to the completeness and correctness of the
recorded transactions every month-end after the last entry in the cashbook in
compliance with the above stated guidelines.

We recommended that the personnel concerned acting as collecting
officers and handled cash accountabilities be issued with fidelity bond based on
the revised schedule of premium rates under Treasury Circular No. 02-2009
dated August 6, 2009 and be issued with an official order designating them as
collecting officers by the Head of Agency prior to their assumption of said
responsibilities pursuant to section 65 volume I of the Government Accounting
and Auditing Manual.

We recommended the management that implementing rules and
regulations agree with the ordinance of the municipality. Further, management
properly administer and implement the scholarship program in accordance with
rules and regulations that will cater all the poor and deserving students of the
municipality to ensure the proper utilization of the scarce resources of the
government.

We recommended that the municipality make an information
dissemination drive to the public regarding their ordinance in the “no
segregation/no collection” policy to protect public health and environment.

We further recommended that the management initiate and coordinate
with the barangay officials in establishing MRF within the barangay and adhere
to the rules and regulations in the establishment of MRF provided under
Sections 32 and 33 of R.A. 9003.

We recommended that the management immediately validate the said
payment deficiencies and settle the same if warranted. Otherwise, management
make representations with the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation by
presenting proof of payment on the supposed unremitted premiums for its
proper disposition.

We recommended that the management submit its justification on noted
deficiency and henceforth ensure that required BAC proceedings are adequately
documented pursuant to the provisions of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-
001.

We recommended that the management verify the lessees’ advance
payment from the market stall occupants and if warranted through sufficient
documentary proof make necessary adjusting entries and henceforth recognize
the same in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.
Otherwise, the management collect the two-month deposit or impose sanctions
for non-compliance thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer submit a justification on the non-submission of the said reports for the said periods and henceforth, comply with the reportorial requirements required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 without prejudice to the administrative sanctions as provided for by applicable laws.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide for the appropriate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements on the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF as set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002. Annex B and D are herein attached for reference.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management submit justification on the
underutilization of the Municipality’s development fund and to evaluate
and assess the Municipality’s current procedure on the implementation
of the 20% development fund so that possible bottlenecks in the
process can be identified and consequently adopt remedial measures to
ensure that priority projects are timely carried out and the fund is
optimally utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy and
effectiveness in the disposition of government resources.

We recommend that Management ensure that the retention money
imposed on payments to contractors is sufficient and in accordance
with the provisions prescribed under R.A. 9184 and its IRR.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee through the BAC
Secretariat ensure that notices or invitations are served to at least three (3)
independent observers with appropriate proof of receipt pursuant to Section
13 of RA 9184.

We recommend that Management speed up the implementation of
said projects and programs funded under the Bottom-Up Budgeting
(BuB) Funds and ensure that funds are optimally utilized with a view of
ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the utilization of
government resources for the benefit of its constituencies.

We recommend that Management submit the approved bidding
documents of the above cited transactions for evaluation by this office
to avoid suspension in audit. Henceforth, we recommend that
Management ensure that all procurements be supported with the duly
approved bidding documents pursuant to the provisions of RA 9184 and
its Implementing Rules and Regulations and to refrain from approving
payments which lack the complete documentary requirements in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend that Management submit its justification on the
failure to prepare the Post-Qualification Evaluation Report.
Henceforth, we recommend that results of the post-qualification
proceedings by the Bids and Award Committee be adequately
supported with the evaluation report pursuant to the provisions of RA
9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend that the Municipality exert more efforts in the
retrieval of loan documents to establish its validity and propriety and
submit the same together with related schedules for evaluation.

Likewise, we recommend that management look for ways to collect from
the borrowers who have remaining loans outstanding balance of
P11,902,598.02 as of December 31, 2017 if warranted.

We recommend that the Chief Executive direct the Inventory
Team/Committee to prepare and submit to the Auditor the inventory
report on the physical count of all existing properties of the Municipality
not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the
NGAS Manual, Volume I to obtain the correct valuation of the property,
plant and equipment. Continued failure on the part of the officials
concerned to submit the reports mentioned shall automatically cause
the suspension of payment of their salaries until they shall have
complied with the requirements of the Commission by virtue of Par. 2,
Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

We recommend that Management submit the Environmental
Clearance Certificate (ECC) pertinent to the above transaction for
evaluation by this office, otherwise a notice of suspension will be
issued to this effect.

Henceforth, we recommend that Management ensure that all
documentary requirements related to acquisition of Real Property are
complied with pursuant to the provisions of RA 8974 and COA Circular
2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend that henceforth, Management require the supplier
to submit a warranty security as prescribed under Section 62 of R.A.
9184 or ensure that a retention money is adequately provided for before
payment of the contract price is released to the supplier to avoid
personal liability of the person responsible for such deficiency.

We recommend that officials responsible for the lapses to submit
a justification and henceforth, ensure that all necessary documents and
needed certifications for the procurement at hand be timely submitted
prior to the issuance of notice of award in adherence to the provisions
of R.A. 9184. Likewise, we recommend that Management review the
data required in its purchase request to conform to the prescribed
standard format pursuant to the government manual for local
government units which necessitates a certification of cash availability.

We recommend that Management submit a justification on the
non-preparation of the required PPMP and submit the duly approved
Amended or Supplemental Procurement Plan, if any, to support the
above transaction.

We also recommend that the BAC require the procuring office to
submit their PPMP and refrain from making procurements without the
required PPMP and duly approved Annual or Supplemental
Procurement Plan otherwise no procurement action shall be undertaken
in adherence to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules
and Regulations to avoid personal liability.

We recommend that Management submit the updated leave cards
and other pertinent documents to support the payment of monetization
of leave credits, otherwise, failure on the part of Management to submit
the required documents will be a ground for issuance of Notice of
Suspension. Henceforth, strict adherence to Civil Service Commission
(CSC) and COA rules and regulations in the processing of monetization
is enjoined to avoid personal liability.

We recommend that Management execute a yearly contract of
lease embodied in a public instrument for every public market stall
occupant outlining the terms and conditions as well as effectivity
thereof to ensure that the interests of both parties are protected
pursuant to COA Circular No. 88-282A. We also recommend that
Management require the stallholders to secure business permit to
legalize their business operation.

We recommend that Management through the Human Resource
Management Office (HRMO) evaluate the Municipality’s present policy, if
any, for the determination of wage rate of its Job Order workers,
otherwise establish appropriate guidelines to ensure that wage rate of
JO workers are in accordance with the minimum standards set forth by
government regulating bodies to avoid being subjected to unfair labor
practices with a view of ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness
in its operation.

We recommend that Management look for ways to convert the
existing open dumpsite into controlled dumpsite as required under
Section 37 of RA 9003 for the purpose of eliminating potential health
hazards and eventually establish a sanitary landfill sites as final
disposal sites for solid and residual wastes of the municipality in
accordance with the guidelines set under Sections 40 and 41 of the
same Act.

We further recommend that Management secure the approval of
the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid
Waste Management Commission and monitor the Safe Closure and
Rehabilitation Plan of the Dumpsite. We too recommend that
Management initiate and coordinate with the barangay officials in
establishing MRF within the barangay and adhere to the rules and
regulations provided under Sections 32 and 33 of R.A. 9003.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor consider that the hiring
of job order personnel be based on necessity and should be
identified/attributed to specific projects for a period not exceeding six
months pursuant to Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules on
Appointments and Other Personnel Actions, under CSC Memorandum
Circular No. 40 and Section 77 of R.A. 7160.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management through the
Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) evaluate the
Municipality’s manpower structure and present system on the hiring of
its Job Order workers and consequently establish appropriate
guidelines or policies to ensure that hiring are in accordance with the
agency’s needs, service requirements, financial capabilities and of the
workers capabilities and qualifications pursuant to provisions set forth
by government regulating bodies with a view of ensuring efficiency,
economy and effectiveness in its operation and the disposition of
government’s scarce resources. Likewise, maintain adequate personnel
records to facilitate easy verification and monitoring of the agency’s
manpower resources.

We recommend that
Management adhere to the
prescribed number of days
as to the period of action on
procurement activities as
well as prohibit the issuance
of notice to proceed for
contractors that failed to post
performance security within
the prescribed period
pursuant to the applicable
provisions of RA 9184 and
the provisions stipulated in
the Philippine Bidding
Documents.

We recommend that
Management adhere to the
procedural rules and
regulations in the
management of funds as
embodied in the Local
Treasury Manual and ensure
that transactions are
properly recorded and
accounted at the time they
occur in accordance with the
generally accepted
accounting principles and
standards.

We recommend that
Management ensure that
vital data appear in the basic
documentary requirements
for disbursement of funds.
We also recommend that
Management make sure that
liquidated damages if
warranted be deducted from
claims of suppliers who have
incurred delays in the
delivery of goods or have
violated the terms stipulated
therein.

We recommend that
Management adhere to the
provisions of the COA
Circular No. 96-010 in the
preparation of basic
supporting documents on
procurement of goods.
We also recommend that
Management submit
explanation for the
inconsistencies in the dates
of Purchase Order, Delivery
Receipt and Inspection and
Acceptance Report of the
subject transactions.

We recommend that the
Management strictly comply
with the provisions of RA
9184 and its IRR in all its
procurement activities and to
submit the Official Receipt
and Certificate of
Registration to ascertain that
the ownership of the
procured equipments had
been transferred in the name
of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accountable officer submit the Memorandum of Agreement duly executed by and between the Department of Agriculture and Municipality of Bantayan, Cebu to determine whether the said project was properly implemented and whether the municipality strictly adhered to the provisions thereof. Furthermore, it is recommended that management render full accounting by submitting reports on the financial operation of the food terminal project during its six (6) months of operation for evaluation of the project.
observations.managementAction: The operating capital of P50,000.00 was already returned to the LGU’s treasury office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the identified officials and employees be required to refund the amount of taxi fares claimed in excess of the allowable amount pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-005.
We further recommend that henceforth, claims for taxi fare while on official travel shall be supported with official receipt to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Claims for taxi fares are now pegged at P75.00 unless a receipt is attached to the voucher of the requisitioner.

observations.recommendation: Management should have refrained from granting additional benefits/extra cash gifts and because it had erred in granting the same it should require the officials and employees concerned to refund the amounts they have illegally received.
observations.managementAction: Collected the whole amount from employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management reevaluate the LDRRMP and ensure that the utilization of the LDRRMF is maximized through the implementation of its programs, projects or activities. Likewise, adherence to the reportorial guidelines on the utilization of LDRRMF is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Hiring of permanent personnel in the DRRM office will be done in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer conduct a complete physical count of properties, prepare and submit to the Auditor an updated inventory report on the physical count of the properties not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The office of the GSO will be created within the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all concerned municipal officials be directed to refund the amount of honorarium received. Henceforth, strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 85-55-A and other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: Granting of Honoraria was already stopped.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the municipality exert more efforts in the retrieval of loan documents to establish their validity and propriety and send demand letters to the borrowers who have outstanding loans amounting to P12,998,548.02 as of December 31, 2014.
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountant to retrieve the records of borrowers, maintain and update the subsidiary ledger for each borrower, monitor strictly payments and send regularly demand letter/bills to the borrowers; and the Treasurer to enforce collections from borrowers, to enable this investment plan to prosper for the benefit of the municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already sent to the borrowers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive send confirmation letters to the debtors to verify the authenticity and collectibility of the receivables and enforce collection thereof. If found no longer collectible, request for authority to write-off from the books of accounts should be initiated to present the correct balance.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already sent to the borrowers.

observations.recommendation: Require the refund of the said Productivity Enhancement Incentive paid to all officials and employees in violation of the aforecited regulation. Furthermore, any review comments by the Provincial Finance Committee on municipal budgets that needs to be complied with strictly must be taken into consideration before disbursing funds to avoid disallowance in post-audit.
observations.managementAction: Granting of PEI was not anymore paid.

observations.recommendation: It is hereby recommended that excess amounts of extra Christmas bonus or extra cash gift should be refunded by those concerned and henceforth the granting of extra cash gift for the ensuing year or years should be up only to the amount of PS limitation as will be regulated by Local Budget Circulars issued as guidelines for the granting of the same.
observations.managementAction: Collected Refund of extra bonus from employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide established guidelines setting forth the types of assistance to be covered, conditions, requirements and parameters of its financial assistance program to ensure reasonableness and equity of the program’s disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Management Guidelines is still in process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that appropriate action will be undertaken to recover the undeposited collections.
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer deposit all collections in her possession to the authorized government depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day pursuant to Section 32 of the NGAS Manual Volume 1 and Section 134 of the GAAM Volume I to avoid misappropriation, borrowing, or loss thru robbery or theft.
The Municipal Treasurer should deposit intact all collections with the authorized depository bank daily or on the next banking day pursuant to Section 32 of the NGAS Manual Volume I, and Section 134 of GAAM Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Complaint was already filed with the Office of the Ombudsman.

observations.recommendation:
We advise that the Municipal Mayor demand the persons liable to remit immediately the collections totalling P692,669.17 to the Municipal Treasurer, who will issue the Official Receipt upon remittance.
Henceforth, the accountable officers are enjoined to issue official receipts to acknowledge receipt of all collections, deposit collections daily and intact to the authorized depository bank pursuant to Sections 29 and 32 of New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual, and refrain from using collections to pay for expenses incurred.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
